SELECT IFNULL(ColumnName,0) FROM TableName. or. SELECT COALESCE(ColumnName,0) FROM TableName.", "score": 0.21895353, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://www.sqlservercentral.com/forums/topic/check-for-null-or-empty-values-and-return-0", "title": "Check for null or empty values and return 0 - SQLServerCentral", "content": "# Check for null or empty values and return 0. I am using the below query to calculate column values. But I need to return zero when a column values is empty or null. [Variance] = SUM([Previous\\_Year]-[Current\\_Year]),. [New Value] = SUM([Previous\\_Year]+[Current\\_Year]). You can use NULLIF and COALESCE to accomplish this. `coalesce(nullif(rtrim(ltrim(column)),''),0)`. Coalesce will return the first non-NULL value, so if the sum Previous\\_Year + Current\\_Year returns a NULL value it will go to the next value in the comma delimited list in this case 0. `[New Value] = coalesce(SUM([Previous_Year]+[Current_Year]),0)`. If you want to return a NULL instead of a certain value, i.e. empty string, you can use NULLIF. Example: `nullif(ltrim(rtrim([Original])),'') AS [Originals],`. If you need more help please read the article in my signature on how to post questions on the forum and please provide the create table statement along with some sample data.
